title: Les Misérables
author: Hugo, Victor
summary: Les Misérables traces Jean Valjean’s journey from convict to moral leader after an act of mercy by Bishop Myriel. Interwoven are Fantine’s downfall and Cosette’s rescue and upbringing; the relentless inspector Javert; the cunning Thénardiers; the streetwise Gavroche; and the idealistic students led by Enjolras and Marius. Hugo spans personal redemption, social injustice, and historical episodes including Waterloo and the 1832 Paris uprising, moving between convent life, prisons, hovels, and barricades. Through rich digressions—legal cases, Latin aphorisms, and urban topography—the novel examines law, grace, poverty, and political ferment in nineteenth‑century France. It juxtaposes intimate suffering with sweeping historical panorama vividly.
